compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Fresh git worktree of rebar (no .rebar/ directory) with the shared ticket store reachable (.tickets-tracker via the common .git).
2. `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_llm_disposition_plumbing.py::test_degraded_code_review_carries_disposition -q`
3. The autouse `_no_repo_root_leaks` fixture ERRORs at teardown: `Test leaked new entries into REPO_ROOT: ['.rebar', '.rebar/current_session_log']`.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: unit tests never touch the real store or the repo root; the suite passes in a fresh worktree.
- Actual: `_degraded_code_review_verdict` -> `rebar.llm.failure.log_degrade` -> `rebar.append_session_log` performs a REAL store write (creates a session_log ticket in the shared tickets branch) and creates the `.rebar/current_session_log` pointer in the repo root. In CI the store is unreachable so the swallowed exception masks it; in a developer worktree it both fails the suite (leak fixture) and pollutes the shared store with test session-log tickets. Found while landing ticket 70c8-423e-e4c1-4d86 (pre-existing: reproduces on pristine origin/main).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/unit/test_llm_disposition_plumbing.py degraded-verdict tests stub or isolate `failure.log_degrade` (or the tests run under an isolated REBAR store root) so no real store write occurs.
- [ ] The full unit suite passes in a fresh worktree whose repo root has no .rebar/ directory.
- [ ] No session_log ticket is created in the store by running the unit suite.
compiled_state.title: log_degrade in unit tests writes to the real ticket store (leaks .rebar/current_session_log into fresh worktrees)
